free_text: Activation cross sections for reactions of Rh and Ru
with 14.7 MeV neutrons.
Neutron source strengths of 10**9 to
5 x 10**10 DT neutrons/sec x 4Pi
Powdered samples of rhodium and ruthenium in
5 ml polystyrene. In irradiations of enriched
ruthenium isotopes, powdered Al(2)O(3) was thoroughly
mixed with each sample prior to irradiation to surmount
the difficulty of exact reproduction of the geometry in
irradiations of milligram quantities of materials.
7.6 cm x 7.6 cm NaI(TI) crystal scintillation
spectrometer and a 400-channel transistorized analyser
Activation
Tbl.1 from NP,75,215
Translated from SCISRS
converted to reaction formalism
SD: Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections according last EXFOR rules and Dict.
REACTION code corrected in Subents 2,4. BIB updated.
The cross section for the 96Ru(n,2n)95Ru reaction was
measured by determining the amount of 20 h 95Tc that
grew in from the 99 min 95Ru during irradiation and
before chemical separation of the technetium fraction
was completed.
The standard deviation is an estimate of
the errors and uncertainties involved in the
measured quantities
SD: REACTION code corrected:
96Ru(n,d)+96Ru(n,n+p) -> 96Ru(n,x). COMMENT deleted.
The cross section for the 96Ru(n,p)96Tc reaction was
calculated assuming a total branching ratio (see
Nuclear Data Sheets) of 2.8 for the integrated peaks
of the three gammas of 96Tc.
The standard deviation is an estimate of
the errors and uncertainties involved in the
measured quantities
SD: COMMENT was added.
The contribution from 100Ru(n,d)99mTc could not be
measured; hence only the total cross section for the
reactions 99Ru(n,p)99mTc+ 100Ru(n,d)99mTc was
determined. Assuming an internal conversion
coefficient (see Nuclear Data Sheets) of 0.095 for the
142 keV gamma this total cross section was determined.
The standard deviation is an estimate of
the errors and uncertainties involved in the
measured quantities
SD: REACTION code corrected:
99Ru(n,p)+100Ru(n,d) -> Ru(n,x). COMMENT was added.
contribution of 104Ru(n,alpha) was taken into account
The standard deviation is an estimate of
the errors and uncertainties involved in the
measured quantities
SD: ANALYSIS, STATUS added. REACTION code
corrected: 101Ru(n,p)+102Ru(n,d) -> Ru(n,x).
The weighted average cross section from two gamma
spectra taken at different times after the end of the
irradiation assuming a branching ratio of 0.91 for
the 0.307 MeV radiation.
The cross section of this reaction was determined by
measuring 14 min 101Tc, which is the beta decay
daughter of 101Mo.
The standard deviation is an estimate of
the errors and uncertainties involved in the
measured quantities
SD: ANALYSIS, COMMENT added.
The standard deviation is an estimate of
the errors and uncertainties involved in the
measured quantities
The thermal neutron yield was not great enough to
affect this result significantly.
SD: ANALYSIS, COMMENT added.
Using a branching ratio of 0.72 for the 0.360 MeV gamma
radiation and assuming no beta decay to the ground
state of 102Ru, the analysis of several 104Tc gamma
spectra taken at different times after the end of the
irradiation and from three separate neutron irradia-
tions gave a weighted average for the cross section.
The standard deviation is an estimate of
the errors and uncertainties involved in the
measured quantities
SD: ANALYSIS added.
The weighted average cross section for this reaction
from three separate neutron irradiations was defined,
using a branching ratio of 0.885 for the 0.498 MeV
gamma radiation.
Since enriched 104Ru containing no 96Ru was used in
these measurements, no 4.3 d 96Tc or 20 h 95Ru was
made in the irradiation.
The standard deviation is an estimate of
the errors and uncertainties involved in the
measured quantities
SD: ANALYSIS, COMMENT added.
The cross section of the 102Ru(n,p)102Tc reaction was
calculated on the basis of the 102Ru level scheme (see
REL-REF) assuming no beta decay to the ground state of
102Ru.  A branching ratio of 1.0 was used for the
0.475 MeV radiation, ignoring the 1.105 MeV crossover
radiation.
From the NP,75, p.221: No attempt was made to detect
any short-lived (5+-1 sec) activity of Tc-102g
The standard deviation is an estimate of
the errors and uncertainties involved in the
measured quantities
SD: Tc-102 -> Tc-102m in REACTION code.
ANALYSIS,REL-REF and COMMENT were added.
Using two separate rhodium irradiations, and separate
monitor runs (copper and aluminium), as well as the
integrated peak areas from several spectra taken up to
8 h after irradiation, the cross section for the
103Rh(n,n'alpha)99mTc reaction is calculated, using a
conversion coefficient (see Nuclear Data Sheets) of
0.095 for the 142 keV radiation.
The standard deviation is an estimate of
the errors and uncertainties involved in the
measured quantities
SD: ANALYSIS and REL-REF were added.
Upper limit determined from Rh-103 (n,n'alpha).
see ANALYSIS
SD: STATUS added
Upper limit determined from Rh-103 (n,n'alpha).
see ANALYSIS
SD: STATUS added
